key_insight: An adaptive, foundation-model-assisted crystal structure prediction workflow can cut DFT labeling cost while maintaining accuracy and discovering new stable phases.
engineering_relevance: This approach makes evolutionary crystal structure prediction more practical for complex multicomponent materials by efficiently focusing expensive quantum calculations on the most informative structures.
method: Start from a pretrained machine-learning interatomic potential (MLIP) to explore candidate crystal structures quickly.
Iteratively select compact, representative, and physically relevant subsets of structures for DFT labeling.
Fine-tune the MLIP self-consistently during evolutionary search to improve system-specific prediction quality.
evidence_signal: The workflow reproduces the known low-pressure convex hull for Ca-Fe-Ni and predicts Ca6FeNi as thermodynamically stable above 100 GPa.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality of the pretrained potential and the selection strategy for which candidate structures are sent to DFT.
paper_title: Adaptive fine-tuning of foundation models for crystal structure prediction: Discovery of high-pressure phases in the CaFeNi system
